transcript: Good morning everyone. I'm here today to give two important product announcement toward Dune vision to be the onchain data platform. But first, Dune is a data and data infrastructure provider founded in 2018 with 60 plus employees around the world now. What our analytics arm does in a nutshell is we ingest, index and store blockchain data for more than 130 chains. We decode the data to make it actionable and we create valuable datasets on top of it for insights to be just few clicks away. Just to give a few examples, what does it mean using Solana as a test case. We have real-time data ingestion. We store one plus petabyte of data, so you don't have to. We have the most extensive DEX trades coverage in the market. We have of course live program decoding. And any of you can submit product... program decoding to us. And this is something we are very proud of because we don't gatekeep what we decode and what we put on our platform, but any team can submit their programs for decoding and use Dune as analytics platform right away. And on top of that, we have also an extensive token prices coverage with more than 700 tokens. How you can access the data? We have three main ways. The Analytics Hub at dune.com is what is Dune is most famous for, but we also have API and Connectors, so that you can query very powerful datasets directly into your stack via API. We'll come back on this a bit later. And Datashare. So we can pull the data... push the data directly into your database, can be Snowflake, Databricks and Google BigQuery. So today, the first announcement that I want to make is that, of course, we've been working with Solana for a long time as a long time partners, but now we are opening through our Catalyst program SVM support for all chains that want to be on Dune. This means that we have a streamlined way to bring your data into Dune and have a full capabilities like raw data, decoded instruction and self CPI logs and curated datasets like DEX trades, token transfer. And I'm beyond excited to announce here today that our first partner in this initiative is the ultra-performant L1 SVM compatible Fogo, whose data is gonna be live on Dune in Q1 2026. So very happy to have Fogo partnering with us. The second update is how you interact with Dune data. So we launched recently our DBT connector, which is a game changer on how you can work with us. This image can very well explain how it works. So the orange part is what we do. So we ingest the data, we create datasets, but to be very flexible, we give you also opportunity to upload your own data and now also to connect any DBT project to Dune warehouse. So what it means is that you connect any project and Dune can be its execution environment. DBT has some obvious advantages such as a very streamlined way to work with Git, you have incremental builds, and Dune can really, really become your onchain data warehouse. So you can say goodbye to your very expensive materialized views and use this new feature that is now available to all our enterprise customers. This is everything I had for you today. And thank you everyone for attending. And just as a reminder, if it's onchain, it's on Dune. Thank you everyone.

sources.title: dbt Labs Expands dbt Fusion Engine Ecosystem with Microsoft Fabric Integration
sources.snippet:
dbt Labs Expands dbt Fusion Engine Ecosystem with Microsoft Fabric Integration | dbt Labs
[Blog](https://www.getdbt.com/blog)
/
[Press](https://www.getdbt.com/blog/category/press)
/
dbt Labs Expands dbt Fusion Engine Ecosystem with Microsoft Fabric Integration
# dbt Labs Expands dbt Fusion Engine Ecosystem with Microsoft Fabric Integration
[Elaine Green](https://www.getdbt.com/authors/elaine-green)
on Nov 18, 2025
*Fusion integration will enable faster, more governed data transformation for analytics and AI workloads*
**PHILADELPHIA –**November 18, 2025 –[dbt Labs](http://getdbt.com), a leader in standards for AI-ready structured data, today announced the expansion of the dbt Fusion engine ecosystem with a new native integration in Fabric Data Factory. The integration, which builds on a longstanding collaboration with Microsoft, will allow users to build, test and orchestrate dbt transformations directly within Microsoft Fabric. The new capability will enable data teams to move faster and improve data governance while enhancing the developer experience and delivering orders of magnitude improvements in developer productivity.
“The speed and complexity of modern analytics and AI projects demand that teams can transform and serve data seamlessly across their stack,” said Ryan Segar, Chief Product Officer at dbt Labs. “By bringing the dbt Fusion engine into Microsoft Fabric, users can now rely on the standard for transformation to deliver trusted, production-grade data and responsibly scale analytics for AI.”
Built on Rust and equipped with deep SQL comprehension, the[dbt Fusion engine](https://www.getdbt.com/product/fusion)is a monumental evolution of the technology that powers dbt. It delivers a significantly enhanced developer experience, all while empowering organizations to operate with the highest quality, context-rich data at scale. With parse times up to 30x faster than dbt Core, Fusion executes large dbt projects in milliseconds instead of minutes. Bringing Fusion into Data Factory will allow teams to seamlessly author, test, and deploy dbt models directly in Fabric, with no Command Line Interface (CLI) setup and no external orchestration. Enterprise security controls via Fabric are automatically applied to every transformation, delivering governance at scale.
“With Microsoft Fabric, teams are building the data products required to deliver trustworthy AI to their organizations,” said Faisal Mohamood, Corporate Vice President at Microsoft. “Embedding dbt into Microsoft Fabric brings together Microsoft’s cloud-scale capabilities with dbt’s transformation framework, helping customers build a robust data foundation required for AI.”
Announced today at Microsoft Ignite 2025, dbt job in Microsoft Fabric is available now as public preview. The initial integration uses dbt Core and will be expanded to include the dbt Fusion engine in 2026.
**About dbt Labs**
Since 2016, dbt Labs has been on a mission to help data practitioners create and disseminate organizational knowledge. dbt is the standard for AI-ready structured data. Powered by the dbt Fusion engine, it unlocks the performance, context, and trust that organizations need to scale analytics in the era of AI. Globally, more than 80,000 data teams use dbt, including those at Siemens, Roche and Condé Nast.
Learn more at getdbt.com, and follow dbt Labs on[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/dbtlabs/mycompany/),[X](https://x.com/dbt_labs),[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/dbt_labs/), and[YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/c/dbt-labs).

sources.title: Nov 06, 2025: dbt Projects on Snowflake ( General availability ) ¶
sources.snippet:
Nov 06, 2025: dbt Projects on Snowflake (General availability) | Snowflake Documentation
# Nov 06, 2025: dbt Projects on Snowflake (*General availability*)[¶](#nov-06-2025-sf-dbt-general-availability)
dbt Projects on Snowflake are now generally available.
dbt Projects on Snowflake let you use familiar Snowflake features to create, edit, test, run, and manage dbt Core projects. You can use Workspaces in
Snowsight to work with dbt project files and directories and deploy a dbt project as a schema-level DBT PROJECT object. You can also
use SQL to work with dbt project objects and use Snowflake CLI commands to integrate deployment and execution into your CI/CD workflows.
## What’s new since preview[¶](#what-s-new-since-preview)
* **Performance improvements and optimizations when running dbt commands on Snowflake:**During preview, result upload usually took approximately
6 to 6.5 minutes. Now, upload completes approximately 8 to 10x faster in \~40 to 45 seconds.
* **Secondary roles requirement:**Using dbt in Workspaces no longer requires secondary roles.
* **Increased coverage for dbt commands:**dbt Projects on Snowflake now support the`dbtdocsgenerate`and`dbtretry`commands and additional
flags in`dbtcompile`.
* **Support in Snowsight:**View the project DAG alongside model and test source code from theProject detailspage.
* **Expanded execution and scheduling UI support:**Enable customers to run and schedule dbt projects from theProject detailspage in
Snowsight.
* **Easy access to dbt execution artifacts:**Easily access the dbt project execution artifacts containing execution results and log files. This
is helpful for debugging dbt project executions and integration with central observability tools.
* **Replication support of dbt project objects:**Replicate dbt project objects to failover accounts.
For more information, see[dbt Projects on Snowflake](../../../user-guide/data-engineering/dbt-projects-on-snowflake).
On this page
1. [What’s new since preview](#what-s-new-since-preview)
Language:**English**

tags.impact_reason: Dune's SVM support and dbt connector enhance data accessibility and development capabilities on Solana, but the impact is moderate as it relies on developer adoption and Fogo's success.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, monolithic Data Warehouse as the anchor because the story's core is Dune's evolution into a production-grade 'data warehouse' and foundational infrastructure. The massive, glowing intake ports represent the 'opening of gates' to new SVM chains, a key announcement. I included the text 'SVM' as a large, diegetic, architectural sign to highlight this new, open standard being adopted by the platform, making it a central feature of this new capability.
visual_idea.description: A low-angle shot looking up at a colossal, brutalist data warehouse that pierces a dark, cavernous void. The structure is made of immense polished concrete slabs and brushed metal. Thousands of glowing fiber optic cables, like rivers of light, flow into a massive, humming intake port on its face. Integrated into the architecture directly above this glowing gateway, the letters 'SVM' are displayed as a stark, white, illuminated sign, signifying this new entrance is open. The background fades into darkness, punctuated by the faint, rhythmic blinking of distant server racks, suggesting infinite depth and storage capacity.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A colossal, brutalist data warehouse, captured in a dramatic low-angle shot looking sharply upwards, dominating a vast, dark, cavernous void. The monumental structure is forged from immense, pristine polished concrete slabs and sections of dark, brushed metal, reflecting subtle light and contributing to a high-contrast cyber-noir atmosphere. Thousands of pulsing, glowing fiber optic cables, resembling vibrant rivers of light, converge and flow into a massive, humming intake port on its face. Integrated directly into the architecture above this intensely glowing gateway, the letters 'SVM' are displayed as a stark, white, intensely illuminated sign, clearly signifying that this new entrance is open. Volumetric lighting dramatically cuts through the deep shadows, enhancing the colossal scale and emphasizing this data monolith's immense presence. The background recedes into an oppressive darkness, faintly punctuated by the rhythmic, distant blinking of countless server racks, subtly hinting at an unimaginable, infinite depth and storage capacity. The composition, set to a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizes the towering height and epic scale, with significant negative space above the structure, creating a sense of awe and monumentality. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-realistic lighting, dramatic lighting, photorealistic, detailed textures, hyperrealistic.

A dense, tightly-packed bundle of thousands of multi-colored, pulsing fiber optic cables streams directly toward the viewer, disappearing into the dark, hexagonal maw of a data intake port. The polished concrete and metal edges of the port catch the intense, shifting light from the cables, highlighting the sheer texture and overwhelming throughput of information. The focus is purely on the raw, kinetic energy of data flow.

editorial.headline: Dune Pivots to Full-Stack Data Warehouse; Opens SVM Gates with Fogo L1
editorial.standfirst: Dune is evolving from an analytics dashboard into a production-grade data warehouse with the launch of a native dbt connector. The platform also announced the expansion of its SVM support via the 'Catalyst' program, onboarding high-performance L1 Fogo as its premier partner.
key_takeaways: **Native dbt Execution:** Enterprise clients can now run dbt projects directly on Dune, enabling git-based workflows and incremental builds without external warehousing.
**SVM Catalyst Program:** Dune has opened its ingestion infrastructure to all SVM chains, allowing standardized data integration for the growing ecosystem of L2s and app chains.
**Fogo Partnership:** The first partner in the Catalyst initiative is **Fogo**, a high-performance SVM L1, with data going live on Dune in Q1 2026.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Dune is fundamentally altering its value proposition for institutional data teams, moving beyond its reputation as a query interface to become a comprehensive onchain data warehouse. The headline announcement from **Solana Breakpoint 2025** is the immediate availability of a native **dbt (data build tool) connector** for enterprise clients. This integration allows engineering teams to treat Dune as their primary execution environment, replacing cumbersome and expensive materialized views with standardized, version-controlled data pipelines. By enabling incremental builds and Git integration directly on the platform, Dune is effectively eliminating the need for teams to export raw blockchain data to **Snowflake** or **BigQuery** simply to perform transformations.

Simultaneously, Dune is positioning itself as the aggregation layer for the fracturing SVM landscape. Through its new **Catalyst program**, the company is opening its infrastructure to ingest data from any SVM-compatible chain. This move addresses a critical bottleneck in the ecosystem: as the number of SVM L2s and app-chains proliferates, liquidity and data fragmentation have become major hurdles for investors and developers. The Catalyst program standardizes this ingestion process, ensuring that new chains can be queryable on Dune from day one rather than waiting for manual integration.

The first beneficiary of this initiative is **Fogo**, a new ultra-performant SVM Layer 1 blockchain. **Fogo** has been confirmed as the inaugural Catalyst partner, with its full dataset—including raw history, decoded instructions, and curated tables—scheduled to go live in Q1 2026. For VCs and institutional analysts, this partnership signals a maturing infrastructure stack where due diligence on new high-throughput chains can happen in real-time, leveraging the same standardized datasets used for Solana Mainnet.
